staged_plan: Stage 1: HOLD all 420.625 sh through Jul 17 record date — selling before record forfeits ~$421 (~22% of current value) with zero compensating gain. Stage 2: Collect $1.00/sh distribution Jul 31 (~$421, tax-free Roth). Stage 3: EXIT 100% of stub in first week of August, before NYSE delisting migrates position to OTC and imposes structural 10-20% illiquidity discount on remaining proceeds. Do NOT add shares at any price — scorecard WAIT holds; the dated catalyst governs only exit sequencing, not accumulation. Invalidation override on Stage 3 only: if stub NAV is independently verified >$4.00/sh AND 4+1 active negotiations close within 60 days AND G&A cuts -35% QoQ, pause Stage 3 and reassess remaining distribution timeline.
dissent_logged: Fundamental (2x weight): NAV unverifiable from available data — cannot price the stub with confidence; GAAP losses and -46.5% op margin confirm overhead is eroding residual value. Technical (2x weight): Setup is dead-cat bounce probable, not recovery; target reclaim of 50d (5.15) is 15% above current, unlikely before delisting. Skeptic: The strongest counter — Stage 3 EXIT — is accepted and embedded in the staged plan. Remaining dissent: stub may have residual upside if negotiations close fast, but size (0.42% of book) does not justify the illiquidity bet.
cio_memo: SITC is a wind-down, not an investment. The only analytically meaningful decision is: (A) EXIT NOW and forfeit $421 in 30 days — negative EV, strictly dominated; (B) HOLD-through-record-then-EXIT — collect near-certain distribution, then end exposure while NYSE liquidity still exists. Holding the OTC stub beyond August fails the Roth opportunity-cost test: $1,455 locked 3-5yr in an illiquid stub at unverified NAV vs 10%/yr Roth compounding foregoes $1,100-1,400. G&A drag (~$8-10M/yr × 4yr) at this market cap scale consumes 14-17% of remaining proceeds regardless of asset sale prices. The Winthrop Realty 2014 analog is instructive: distributions came in 12-20% below initial NAV estimates and the timeline overran, both of which are base-case risks here. Smart-money arb stakes (Rush Island 7.6%, Gumshoe 5.1%) are passive, index-arb driven — not a signal of undiscovered value. At 0.42% of book, this is a process question, not a capital question. Process: HOLD 30 days, collect, exit clean. Conviction scoring: base 3, catalyst confirmed +1 (Jul 31 payment), LATE narrative -1, SM accumulating +1, delisting/illiquidity risk -1 = 3.
circle_of_competence: SITC is no longer a REIT — it is a liquidating vehicle in terminal wind-down, selling assets and returning capital via special dividends toward NYSE delisting and statutory dissolution. The return driver is the spread between current price and total remaining distributions, net of G&A overhead and timeline overruns before dissolution completes.
skeptic_response: The strongest argument is the combination of Roth opportunity cost ($1,100-1,400 in foregone 10%/yr compounding over 3-5yr) plus OTC delisting trap (10-20% structural discount after NYSE exit), which together make holding the stub beyond the next dividend irrational. I accept this fully — for the stub. The argument does not apply to the next 30-day window. Forfeiting the Jul 31 $1.00/sh ($421) to exit today is strictly negative EV: there is no compensating gain from selling before record date Jul 17, and $421 in 30 days at near-zero execution risk dwarfs any reinvestment alternative at this holding size. The skeptic's logic correctly identifies the exit trigger — immediately post-payment — not pre-payment. Stage 3 EXIT in early August addresses both the opportunity-cost and the OTC-trap concerns simultaneously.
